tender.title: Together Housing Extra Care
tender.description: The service will support clients to lead independent and fulfilling lives, promote effective personal support networks enabling them to successfully maintain their own home, within the community and their chosen way of life. The provision of person-centred support will reduce or delay the need for care provision, including residential or nursing accommodation, and lessen the need for crisis intervention, including health and social care provisions.

tender.description: The London Borough of Havering (the Council) is commissioning a Care & Well-Being Support Service in the borough's three Extra Care Schemes for a three year contract (+1 year +1 year extension options) with a commencement date of 1st December 2023. The Council is seeking to commission one provider to deliver this service across the three Extra Care schemes in Havering to deliver efficiencies.

tender.title: Supported Living with Accommodation- Sub-Lot 3e
tender.description:
The Royal Borough of Kingston upon Thames is looking for Supported Living services, support and accommodation for 6 service users to be in place by 1st of April 2022.\r
\r
The call-off contract term will reflect the framework terms and end on 30/06/26, noting that there are also two 12 months extensions available, which may be invoked at the sole discretion of the Council (making the maximum possible end date as 30th June 2028.)

tender.title: AMCV 314 - Floating Housing Support including Housing Advice and Prevention of Homelessness
tender.description:
This is an award notice for  Housing Support services that will support people whose needs are such that their ability to maintain a tenancy or remain independent in their home would be compromised without that support. The focus of housing-related support will be on ensuring that people have access to appropriate and settled accommodation according to their circumstances and have the support networks in place to help them sustain that accommodation. 
      The total budget available is capped at £540,000 per annum and will be fixed for the duration of the contract. The duration of the contract will be for an initial term of 3 years with an option to extend the contact by up to a further 2 years subject to contract performance and budget availability.

tender.title: Homes England - Strategic Research and Economic Analysis - Evaluation of the Private Rented Sector and Affordable Housing Guarantees Schemes
tender.description: Homes England and Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) are committed to completing an ambitious and comprehensive evaluation of interventions that the Agency is involved in delivering, to better understand the impact that the Agency has through its market-facing interventions, and to learn lessons from delivery to date to inform future delivery. This study will evaluate the impact and value for money of the Private Rented Sector (PRS) and Affordable Housing (AH) guarantee schemes, as well as evaluate the process and delivery of the programme, providing lessons learnt for the design and delivery of future schemes. The T&Cs for Homes England Frameworks can be found at the following link: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/homes-england-contracts-and-tenders

tender.title: NCC1019 Supported Accommodation and Lodgings for Care Leavers and Young Homeless
tender.description:
Northumberland County Council is seeking to create a Flexible Purchasing Solution (FPS) to develop housing and support services for vulnerable young people aged 16 to 25.  Whilst the overall aim is to prevent youth homelessness and a planned pathway to independent living, there is a specific focus on 16 to 21 year-old care leavers and young people aged 16 or 17 who are homeless and deemed to be Children in Need. This includes Unaccompanied Asylum Seeking young people.\r
\r
The procurement approach is following the Light Touch Regime under the Public Contracts Regulations 2015 Open Procedure.

tender.title: Homes England - Strategic Research and Economic Analysis Framework - Affordable Housing - Fiscal and Household Finance Impacts
tender.description:
Economic appraisal involves the assessment of a range of costs and benefits as defined in the HM Treasury Green Book. An important part of this assessment involves the estimation of fiscal and financial impacts to the public sector, private sector and to households. This commission is concerned with the assessment of fiscal and household financial impacts in the context of the affordable housing products supported by Homes England.\r
The successful supplier will need to work with Homes England to assess the current evidence used within the economic appraisal of affordable housing and to undertake a literature review to determine whether a broader and/or better range of evidence may be utilised.\r
\r
https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/homes-england-contracts-and-tenders
